```{r fig-interaction-policyknow}
                                newdata = datagrid(policyknow_score = policyknow_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Friends Important")

policyknow_slopes <- bind_rows(
  slopes_pok_trust_qc, slopes_pok_trust_can,
  slopes_pok_attach_qc, slopes_pok_attach_can, slopes_pok_attach_city,
  slopes_pok_financial, slopes_pok_life,
  slopes_pok_qcvalues, slopes_pok_qcfriends
)

ggplot(policyknow_slopes, aes(x = policyknow_score, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkgoldenrod") +
  geom_line(color = "darkgoldenrod", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:6) +
  labs(
    x = "Policy Knowledge Score (0-6)",
    y = "Marginal Effect of Payment Proximity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

@fig-interaction-years-ccb, @fig-interaction-income-ccb, and @fig-interaction-french-ccb replicate the heterogeneity analyses for exclusive CCB recipients, where identification is cleanest.

```{r fig-interaction-years-ccb}
#| label: fig-interaction-years-ccb
#| fig-cap: "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity by Years in Quebec (Exclusive CCB Recipients)"
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 12

# Get CCB-specific years range for plotting
years_range_ccb <- range(df_ccb_years$years_in_qc)
years_seq_ccb <- seq(years_range_ccb[1], years_range_ccb[2], by = 1)

# Estimate interaction models explicitly for each DV (CCB sample, using prox_20)
mod_ccb_trust_qc <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_trust_can <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_attach_qc <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_attach_can <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_attach_city <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_financial <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_life <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_qcvalues <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)
mod_ccb_qcfriends <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_20 * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_ccb_years)

# Extract marginal effect of prox_20 at each value of years_in_qc using slopes()
slopes_ccb_trust_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_trust_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                               newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: QC Govt")

slopes_ccb_trust_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_trust_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: Canada Govt")

slopes_ccb_attach_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_attach_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                                newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Quebec")

slopes_ccb_attach_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_attach_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                 newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Canada")

slopes_ccb_attach_city <- slopes(mod_ccb_attach_city, variables = "prox_20",
                                  newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: City")

slopes_ccb_financial <- slopes(mod_ccb_financial, variables = "prox_20",
                                newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Financial Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_life <- slopes(mod_ccb_life, variables = "prox_20",
                           newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Life Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_qcvalues <- slopes(mod_ccb_qcvalues, variables = "prox_20",
                               newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Values Important")

slopes_ccb_qcfriends <- slopes(mod_ccb_qcfriends, variables = "prox_20",
                                newdata = datagrid(years_in_qc = years_seq_ccb)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Friends Important")

# Combine all slopes
years_slopes_ccb <- bind_rows(
  slopes_ccb_trust_qc, slopes_ccb_trust_can,
  slopes_ccb_attach_qc, slopes_ccb_attach_can, slopes_ccb_attach_city,
  slopes_ccb_financial, slopes_ccb_life,
  slopes_ccb_qcvalues, slopes_ccb_qcfriends
)

# Plot
ggplot(years_slopes_ccb, aes(x = years_in_qc, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Years in Quebec",
    y = "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

```{r fig-interaction-income-ccb}
#| label: fig-interaction-income-ccb
#| fig-cap: "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity by Household Income (Exclusive CCB Recipients)"
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 12

# Estimate interaction models explicitly for each DV (CCB sample, using prox_20)
mod_ccb_inc_trust_qc <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_trust_can <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_attach_qc <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_attach_can <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_attach_city <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_financial <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_life <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_qcvalues <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)
mod_ccb_inc_qcfriends <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_20 * income_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_income)

# Extract marginal effect of prox_20 at each income level using slopes()
slopes_ccb_inc_trust_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_trust_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: QC Govt")

slopes_ccb_inc_trust_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_trust_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                    newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: Canada Govt")

slopes_ccb_inc_attach_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_attach_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                                    newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Quebec")

slopes_ccb_inc_attach_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_attach_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                     newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Canada")

slopes_ccb_inc_attach_city <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_attach_city, variables = "prox_20",
                                      newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: City")

slopes_ccb_inc_financial <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_financial, variables = "prox_20",
                                    newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Financial Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_inc_life <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_life, variables = "prox_20",
                               newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Life Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_inc_qcvalues <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_qcvalues, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Values Important")

slopes_ccb_inc_qcfriends <- slopes(mod_ccb_inc_qcfriends, variables = "prox_20",
                                    newdata = datagrid(income_filled = income_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Friends Important")

# Combine all slopes
income_slopes_ccb <- bind_rows(
  slopes_ccb_inc_trust_qc, slopes_ccb_inc_trust_can,
  slopes_ccb_inc_attach_qc, slopes_ccb_inc_attach_can, slopes_ccb_inc_attach_city,
  slopes_ccb_inc_financial, slopes_ccb_inc_life,
  slopes_ccb_inc_qcvalues, slopes_ccb_inc_qcfriends
)

# Plot
ggplot(income_slopes_ccb, aes(x = income_filled, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkgreen") +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8, labels = income_labels) +
  labs(
    x = "Household Income",
    y = "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)
  )
```

```{r fig-interaction-french-ccb}
#| label: fig-interaction-french-ccb
#| fig-cap: "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity by French Competency (Exclusive CCB Recipients)"
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 12

# Estimate interaction models explicitly for each DV (CCB sample, using prox_20)
mod_ccb_fr_trust_qc <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_trust_can <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_attach_qc <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_attach_can <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_attach_city <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_financial <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_life <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_qcvalues <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)
mod_ccb_fr_qcfriends <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_20 * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_ccb_french)

# Extract marginal effect of prox_20 at each French competency level
slopes_ccb_fr_trust_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_trust_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                                  newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: QC Govt")

slopes_ccb_fr_trust_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_trust_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Trust: Canada Govt")

slopes_ccb_fr_attach_qc <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_attach_qc, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Quebec")

slopes_ccb_fr_attach_can <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_attach_can, variables = "prox_20",
                                    newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: Canada")

slopes_ccb_fr_attach_city <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_attach_city, variables = "prox_20",
                                     newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Attachment: City")

slopes_ccb_fr_financial <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_financial, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Financial Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_fr_life <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_life, variables = "prox_20",
                              newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "Life Satisfaction")

slopes_ccb_fr_qcvalues <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_qcvalues, variables = "prox_20",
                                  newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Values Important")

slopes_ccb_fr_qcfriends <- slopes(mod_ccb_fr_qcfriends, variables = "prox_20",
                                   newdata = datagrid(french_comp_filled = french_seq)) |>
  as_tibble() |> mutate(outcome = "QC Friends Important")

# Combine all slopes
french_slopes_ccb <- bind_rows(
  slopes_ccb_fr_trust_qc, slopes_ccb_fr_trust_can,
  slopes_ccb_fr_attach_qc, slopes_ccb_fr_attach_can, slopes_ccb_fr_attach_city,
  slopes_ccb_fr_financial, slopes_ccb_fr_life,
  slopes_ccb_fr_qcvalues, slopes_ccb_fr_qcfriends
)

# Plot
ggplot(french_slopes_ccb, aes(x = french_comp_filled, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
  geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +
  labs(
    x = "French Competency (0-10)",
    y = "Marginal Effect of CCB Payment Proximity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

# Discussion

This paper provides evidence that government benefit payments shape immigrants' political attitudes toward their host society. Using quasi-random variation in survey timing relative to fixed payment dates, I find that proximity to payday increases immigrants' attachment to Quebec, their financial satisfaction, and the importance they place on Quebec values and friendships. These effects are robust to multiple placebo tests and appear specific to immigrant populations.

The findings contribute to several literatures. First, they extend work on policy feedback effects [@campbell2012; @mettler2011] to immigrant populations, showing that welfare state policies may facilitate political integration through repeated material signals of state support. Second, they speak to debates about economic voting and credit attribution [@healy2017; @margalit2013], demonstrating that even relatively small, routine government transfers can shape political orientations when recipients connect benefits to government. Third, they contribute to research on immigrant political incorporation [@dinesen2012; @just2017], identifying a previously unexplored mechanism by which host societies may foster immigrant attachment.

The results also carry implications for contemporary debates about immigrant access to welfare. Welfare chauvinism rests on an implicit cost-benefit calculation: that benefits extended to immigrants yield returns only for the immigrants themselves, at the expense of native taxpayers. This framing ignores potential positive externalities. If benefit receipt builds political belonging, then welfare access may generate precisely the outcomes that receiving societies seek from immigrants—attachment to the host polity, adoption of its values, and investment in social relationships with natives. The broader implication is that integration policy need not rely solely on explicit programming like language classes or civic education; the everyday experience of receiving state support may itself constitute a powerful, if unrecognized, integration mechanism. Countries that restrict immigrant welfare access in the name of fiscal prudence may inadvertently undermine political incorporation.

Several limitations warrant discussion. The analysis cannot identify precisely *why* payment proximity affects attitudes—whether through gratitude, financial security reducing anxiety, or increased salience of government's role in daily life. The effects, while statistically significant, are modest in magnitude. And the Quebec context, with its robust welfare state and distinctive political dynamics around belonging, may not generalize to other settings.

Nevertheless, the results suggest that the welfare state may serve as a mechanism of immigrant political integration—not through explicit citizenship requirements or civic education, but through the quotidian experience of receiving government support. Each benefit payment represents a tangible demonstration that the host society provides for newcomers, potentially building the material foundations of political belonging.

Future research could productively explore several directions. First, examining which specific mechanisms drive the effect—gratitude, financial security, or government salience—would help clarify the theoretical channel. Second, testing whether similar patterns hold in contexts with less generous welfare states or different political cultures would establish generalizability. Third, investigating whether short-term attitude effects translate into longer-term behavioral outcomes, such as civic participation or naturalization, would establish the political significance of these findings. Finally, comparing the integration effects of different policy instruments—cash transfers versus in-kind benefits, universal versus targeted programs—could inform policy design.

\newpage

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

\newpage

# Appendix {.unnumbered}

## Wrong-Date Placebo: Immigrant Beneficiaries {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-placebo-wrong presents the wrong-date placebo test for immigrant beneficiaries. For each beneficiary, proximity is calculated to a payment date they do *not* receive. Null effects indicate that the actual payment date specifically matters.

```{r tbl-app-placebo-wrong}
#| label: tbl-app-placebo-wrong
#| tbl-cap: "Wrong-Date Placebo Test (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = p1, "CAN" = p2,
    "QC" = p3, "CAN" = p4, "City" = p5,
    "Financial" = p6, "Life" = p7,
    "Values" = p8, "Friends" = p9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c("prox_wrong" = "Proximity (Wrong Date)"),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Wrong-Date Placebo: Non-Immigrant Beneficiaries {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-nat-placebo presents the wrong-date placebo test for non-immigrant beneficiaries.

```{r tbl-app-nat-placebo}
#| label: tbl-app-nat-placebo
#| tbl-cap: "Wrong-Date Placebo Test (Non-Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

np1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)
np9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_wrong | ID, data = df_nat)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = np1, "CAN" = np2,
    "QC" = np3, "CAN" = np4, "City" = np5,
    "Financial" = np6, "Life" = np7,
    "Values" = np8, "Friends" = np9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c("prox_wrong" = "Proximity (Wrong Date)"),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: Years in Quebec {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-years presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × years in Quebec. A significant interaction term indicates that the effect of payment proximity varies by time spent in Quebec.

```{r tbl-app-interaction-years}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-years
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × Years in Quebec (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

# Estimate interaction models
int_y1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)
int_y9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * years_in_qc | ID, data = df_imm_years)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_y1, "CAN" = int_y2,
    "QC" = int_y3, "CAN" = int_y4, "City" = int_y5,
    "Financial" = int_y6, "Life" = int_y7,
    "Values" = int_y8, "Friends" = int_y9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:years_in_qc" = "Proximity × Years in QC"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: Household Income {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-income presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × household income. Household income is coded on an 8-point scale from 1 (no income) to 8 (\>\$200,000). A significant interaction term indicates that the effect of payment proximity varies by income level.

```{r tbl-app-interaction-income}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-income
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × Household Income (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

# Estimate interaction models
int_i1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)
int_i9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * income_filled | ID, data = df_imm_income)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_i1, "CAN" = int_i2,
    "QC" = int_i3, "CAN" = int_i4, "City" = int_i5,
    "Financial" = int_i6, "Life" = int_i7,
    "Values" = int_i8, "Friends" = int_i9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:income_filled" = "Proximity × HH Income"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: French Competency {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-french presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × French competency. French competency is self-reported on a 0-10 scale. A significant negative interaction term would indicate that payment proximity effects are stronger at lower levels of French competency.

```{r tbl-app-interaction-french}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-french
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × French Competency (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

# Estimate interaction models
int_f1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)
int_f9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * french_comp_filled | ID, data = df_imm_french)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_f1, "CAN" = int_f2,
    "QC" = int_f3, "CAN" = int_f4, "City" = int_f5,
    "Financial" = int_f6, "Life" = int_f7,
    "Values" = int_f8, "Friends" = int_f9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:french_comp_filled" = "Proximity × French Competency"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: Education {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-educ presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × education level. Education is coded on a 1-7 scale (1 = no education, 7 = PhD/professional degree).

```{r tbl-app-interaction-educ}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-educ
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × Education (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

int_ed1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)
int_ed9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * education_num_filled | ID, data = df_imm_educ)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_ed1, "CAN" = int_ed2,
    "QC" = int_ed3, "CAN" = int_ed4, "City" = int_ed5,
    "Financial" = int_ed6, "Life" = int_ed7,
    "Values" = int_ed8, "Friends" = int_ed9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:education_num_filled" = "Proximity × Education"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: Political Knowledge {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-polknow presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × political knowledge score. Political knowledge is measured as the number of politicians correctly identified (0-4 scale).

```{r tbl-app-interaction-polknow}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-polknow
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × Political Knowledge (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

int_pk1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)
int_pk9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * polknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_polknow)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_pk1, "CAN" = int_pk2,
    "QC" = int_pk3, "CAN" = int_pk4, "City" = int_pk5,
    "Financial" = int_pk6, "Life" = int_pk7,
    "Values" = int_pk8, "Friends" = int_pk9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:polknow_score" = "Proximity × Pol. Knowledge"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```

## Interaction Models: Policy Knowledge {.unnumbered}

@tbl-app-interaction-policyknow presents the full interaction model coefficients for payment proximity × policy knowledge score. Policy knowledge is measured as the number of policy questions answered correctly (0-6 scale).

```{r tbl-app-interaction-policyknow}
#| label: tbl-app-interaction-policyknow
#| tbl-cap: "Interaction Effects: Payment Proximity × Policy Knowledge (Immigrant Beneficiaries)"

int_pok1 <- feols(trust_qc ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok2 <- feols(trust_can ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok3 <- feols(attachment_qc ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok4 <- feols(attachment_can ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok5 <- feols(attachment_city ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok6 <- feols(financial_satisf ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok7 <- feols(life_satisf ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok8 <- feols(imp_qcvalues ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)
int_pok9 <- feols(imp_qcfriends ~ prox_actual * policyknow_score | ID, data = df_imm_policyknow)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "QC" = int_pok1, "CAN" = int_pok2,
    "QC" = int_pok3, "CAN" = int_pok4, "City" = int_pok5,
    "Financial" = int_pok6, "Life" = int_pok7,
    "Values" = int_pok8, "Friends" = int_pok9
  ),
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = c(
    "prox_actual" = "Proximity",
    "prox_actual:policyknow_score" = "Proximity × Policy Knowledge"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "FE: ID"),
  output = "tinytable"
) |>
  group_tt(j = list("Trust in\ngovt. of:" = 2:3, "Attachment\nto:" = 4:6, "Satisfaction\nwith:" = 7:8, "Importance of QC" = 9:10)) |>
  theme_tt("striped") |>
  theme_tt("resize") |>
  style_tt(j = 1, bold = TRUE) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = 0.8)
```
